summary: Senior Full-Stack Engineer with 12+ years building product-facing and web security-adjacent systems across SaaS, marketplaces, Web3 security, and enterprise platforms. Brings the Teleport stack overlap in Go, TypeScript, and React: Go/Kafka catalog and onboarding systems at Rappi, React/TypeScript auditor and client workflows at OpenZeppelin, and backend API/data work that improved Premise API response times by 35%. Open-source contributor with Rust-based OSS Relayer ownership, KMS signer integrations, nonce-safety work, Playwright/Jest/Cypress testing, documentation, and direct enterprise-user migration support.

workExperience.title: Software Engineer
description: Co-led Defender 2.0 Secure Audit as one of two engineers, building TypeScript and React workflows that helped smart contract auditors work with clients, track findings, verify fixes, and deliver final PDF reports.

Owned the EVM side of the Rust-based OpenZeppelin Relayer OSS work, including transaction cost handling, RPC retry and failover paths, AWS KMS and GCP KMS signer integrations, and a critical nonce race condition fix.
Built SDK capabilities for Defender and OSS Relayer so Web3 developers and technical teams could integrate security tooling and build custom automation workflows.

Supported the Defender sunset by closing feature gaps, improving documentation, and working directly with enterprise users during migration to OSS Relayer, which covered most enterprise Defender users.
Led Playwright end-to-end testing for Defender critical user flows, improving release confidence for security-sensitive product workflows.
Refactored the TypeScript monorepo build system through ESM migration, package exports, bundler and tsconfig updates, reducing local and CI build times from about 15 minutes to 3 minutes.

workExperience.title: Software Engineer
description: Built a client-facing MyPremise feature with React and TypeScript that surfaced live data through one shareable URL instead of repeated PDF or PPT exports.

Contributed to the REST-to-GraphQL migration with Apollo across key entities, including Express.js service updates, reducing endpoint churn, enabling more flexible data retrieval, and improving API response times by 35%.
Improved release reliability by expanding Jest coverage and adding GitHub Actions quality gates, reducing release cycles from one week to less than 24 hours.
Helped standardize the UI with Material UI and Storybook for shared component documentation and design handoff, reducing prototyping time from one week to one day.

workExperience.title: Software Engineer
description: Led development of an internal integration and onboarding platform with Go, Node.js, and Angular, removing dependency on external engineering support, saving $75,000 annually, and giving customer service teams a faster onboarding path.

Implemented real-time catalog and pricing updates with Go and Kafka, migrating core business logic from a Python ETL flow so product and discount changes reached the app faster and contributed to a 21% CSAT increase.

Built backend integrations for merchant ingestion pipelines, normalizing data from FTP, APIs, SOAP XML feeds, and web scraping to support pricing, stock, availability, bundles, and product attributes.
Engineered a Redis-based conflict resolution layer for duplicate and out-of-order updates, improving product data accuracy and reliability by 40%.
Drove unit testing for critical pricing, offers, and bundles logic with an 80% coverage target, reducing critical rollout defects by 33%.

workExperience.title: System Architect
description: Worked with product, engineering, and infrastructure teams to simplify release flow and reduce delivery cycles from months to about two weeks.
Modernized key parts of the stack by moving from ASP.NET to Angular and from .NET Framework to .NET Core, removing Internet Explorer-only constraints and enabling Chrome compatibility.
Implemented transaction-related API flows with Express.js, including integrations with Transbank for payment processing use cases.
Introduced DevOps practices and led a staged migration from on-prem to Azure hybrid infrastructure across applications, databases, jobs, storage, and networking.

workExperience.title: Full Stack Developer
description: Delivered more than five web and mobile products for clients across medical, industrial, government, legal, and cultural sectors, working end to end from discovery and requirements to planning and implementation.
Led architecture decisions for each project and set engineering standards for TypeScript, React, and React Native across the company.

Worked directly with clients through discovery calls, demos, and status updates, including delivery of the PIIA project under a short deadline that helped open government opportunities for the agency.
Mentored five developers through pairing sessions, code reviews, and workshops focused on architecture, testing, and code quality.

workExperience.title: Fullstack Developer
description: Contributed to building Dxpress from idea to launch as one of the first Uber Eats-style delivery services in Chile, working with a small product and engineering team inside an investment firm.
Built Python and Go backend services for advanced search, geolocation, real-time operational features, and product ETL workflows supporting catalogs with more than 5,000 products.

workExperience.title: Junior Software Developer
description: Started as a junior developer and took on growing frontend ownership, helping introduce Node.js and AngularJS into the agency stack.

education.degree: Bachelor of Engineering in Computer Science

targetRole: Senior Full Stack Engineer - Teleport Connect

company_profile.description: Teleport is the AI Infrastructure Identity Company, modernizing identity, access, and policy for infrastructure so engineering teams can move faster while improving resiliency against identity-based compromise.
company_profile.mission: Make trusted computing simple by giving humans, machines, workloads, and AI agents cryptographically secured identities with no shared secrets or standing privilege.
company_profile.vision: Make infrastructure trustworthy through unified identity, zero trust access, identity governance, and machine, workload, and agent identity.
services: Passwordless authentication and ephemeral least-privileged access
Infrastructure identity for AI agents and MCP tools
Secure infrastructure access for engineering teams
company_profile.industry: Infrastructure identity, zero trust access, and identity-based security for cloud, infrastructure, and AI systems.
company_profile.business_model: Open source Teleport project with commercial cloud, self-hosted, enterprise, and sales-led infrastructure identity offerings.
recent_signals: Teleport positions itself as the AI Infrastructure Identity Company for humans, machines, workloads, and AI agents.
The platform emphasizes cryptographic identity, ephemeral privileges, no shared secrets, and no standing privilege.
Teleport highlights 20k GitHub stars and 5000+ community members for its open source project.
The Agentic AI offering treats engineers, machines, LLM tools, AI agents, MCP servers, and digital twins as first-class identities.
Teleport launched Beams as trusted ephemeral runtimes for infrastructure agents.
Teleport states it contracts and publishes third-party security audits annually and maintains SOC 2 Type II, ISO 27001, and HIPAA Security Rule compliance for cloud and self-hosted products.

source_notes.claim: Teleport unifies identities for humans, machines, and AI with strong identity implementation to speed engineering and improve resilience against identity-based attacks.
Teleport establishes a unified identity layer secured cryptographically, with no shared secrets, no standing privilege, and no anonymity.
Teleport describes its vision as making infrastructure trustworthy and trusted computing simple.
Teleport culture behaviors include writing things down, starting simple and iterating, being drivers, being builders, lifting each other up, assuming charity, and valuing time.
Teleport platform products include Zero Trust Access, Identity Governance, Machine & Workload Identity, and Identity Security.

Teleport Zero Trust Access includes passwordless authentication, ephemeral least-privileged access, certificate-based authentication, access governance and compliance, and elimination of VPNs and bastions.
Teleport Agentic AI gives every user, machine, and agent a cryptographic identity and governed access path across GPU clouds, Kubernetes clusters, model registries, and agentic workflows.
Teleport Beams are trusted ephemeral runtimes for infrastructure agents with isolated VMs, delegated identity, and audit trails.
Teleport security commitments include annual third-party security audits, vulnerability scanning, incident response, SOC 2 Type II, ISO 27001, and HIPAA Security Rule compliance.
The official careers page links to Ashby for current openings, and the Ashby API returned 25 listed roles.
The likely matching target opening is Senior Full Stack Engineer - Teleport Connect on Ashby.

The user-provided JD describes a product-focused full-stack role building Teleport UI and backend APIs with Go or Rust, TypeScript, React, RFDs, testing, docs, Teleport Connect, server-side pagination, onboarding, and passwordless UI.
source_notes.source_url: user-provided job description and matching Ashby role page
